# cc-to-grok-bridge

> Thin bridge so Grok Build reuses Claude Code (CC) assets: rules, skills, hooks, memory mirror, MCP reinstall guidance.

## Facts

- System Prompt: same workspace CLAUDE.md (compat). skill: ~/.claude/commands (separate from system prompt).
- Plugins: CC enabledPlugins / SessionStart always-on do not auto-port. Always-on only: A = native grok plugin install when packaging exists; B = .grok/rules auto-load. Do not recommend slash-only as migration. See docs/06_plugins.md.
- Hooks: thin adapter for payload field names and deny protocol; CC hook script bodies stay source of truth.
- Memory: day-use is high-fit — memory_sync + .grok/rules/cc-memory-pointer (loads with workspace rules). Product memory search is optional; bridge does not require [memory] enabled=true just to load the pointer.
- Memory layout: _from_cc / general / grok isolation; optional memory_push for general/ only.
- MCP: never auto-copy secrets. Reinstall by type (API-key HTTP, OAuth, stdio). claude.ai cloud connectors are not portable. Notion and Google handoff in docs/03_mcp.md and AGENTS.md — AI runs commands; user does browser consent.
- In practice, CC → Grok Build is usually smoother than Antigravity/Gemini bridges.

## Commands

- python3 scripts/install_bridge.py
- python3 scripts/memory_sync.py --workspace ~/path/to/your-workspace
- python3 scripts/bridge_doctor.py --workspace ~/path/to/your-workspace
- python3 scripts/hook_acceptance.py --with-clasp

## Docs

- [README.md](README.md): English overview, compatibility matrix
- [README.zh-TW.md](README.zh-TW.md): Traditional Chinese
- [README.zh-CN.md](README.zh-CN.md): Simplified Chinese
- [architecture.md](architecture.md): design invariants
- [AGENTS.md](AGENTS.md): AI install playbooks (MCP Notion/Google/OAuth)
- [docs/02_memory.md](docs/02_memory.md): memory
- [docs/03_mcp.md](docs/03_mcp.md): MCP types
- [docs/06_plugins.md](docs/06_plugins.md): plugins gap and migration
